untranslatable_reason: Pattern requires visual identification of: (1) rising trend line connecting lows with specific angle constraints (30-45 degrees), (2) lead-in phase with vertical price range measurement ($1-$2+) from highest high to trend line, (3) measurement timing during first quarter of formation, (4) sharp upward bump phase detection. These require trend line calculation, geometric angle measurement, and multi-phase pattern recognition that cannot be expressed using only the allowed point-in-time indicators (price, moving averages, RSI, ATR, etc.). No indicator in the allowed set can measure trend line slopes, calculate distances from price to a fitted line, or identify multi-phase chart patterns.
translation_notes: The Bump pattern is fundamentally a visual/geometric chart pattern requiring trend line analysis and angle measurement. Substituted with a basic uptrend condition (price > SMA_20) as a weak proxy, but this does not capture the pattern's true characteristics: the rising trend line connecting lows, the lead-in section with specific vertical range, or the bump phase onset. A proper implementation would require custom trend line detection, line-to-price distance calculation, and phase detection logic outside the scope of allowed indicators.
